question_text: What is the difference between an elastic change and a plastic change in a material?
correct_answer: An elastic change is temporary and the material returns to its original shape and size when the force is removed. A plastic change is permanent and the material keeps its new shape even after the force is removed.
explanation: Stretching a rubber band and releasing it is an elastic change—it springs back. Bending a paperclip is a plastic change—it stays bent. Most materials can undergo both types of change depending on how much force is applied.
